channel.title: CSOH - Cloud Security News
channel.description: Latest cloud security news curated by Cloud Security Office Hours. Covers AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes vulnerabilities, breaches, and more.

item.title: Chinese APT Abuses Multiple Cloud Tools to Spy on Mongolia
item.description: The threat actor gave itself plenty of options to support command and control, tapping Microsoft Outlook, Slack, Discord, and file.io for online espionage.

item.title: fast16 | Mystery ShadowBrokers Reference Reveals High-Precision Software Sabotage 5 Years Before Stuxnet
item.description: A previously unknown 2005 cyber sabotage framework patches high-precision calculation software in memory to silently corrupt results.

item.title: Hackers exploit file upload bug in Breeze Cache WordPress plugin
item.description: Hackers are actively exploiting a critical vulnerability in the Breeze Cache plugin for WordPress that allows uploading arbitrary files on the server without authentication. [...]

item.title: Xinference PyPI package compromise leads to full environment takeover
item.description: A critical supply chain compromise (no CVE assigned) was disclosed affecting the Xinference Python ecosystem, allowing attackers to execute arbitrary code and fully compromise developer environments via malicious pack...

item.title: Checkmarx supply chain compromise exposes CI/CD secrets
item.description: A critical supply chain vulnerability (CVE-2026-33634, CVSS 9.4) was disclosed affecting Checkmarx GitHub Actions and developer tooling, allowing attackers to steal sensitive credentials from CI/CD environments via po...

item.title: Frontier AI and the Future of Defense: Your Top Questions Answered
item.description: What are the next steps for security leaders in this new age of frontier AI? We answer the top 10 questions customers are asking. The post Frontier AI and the Future of Defense: Your Top Questions Answered appeared fi...

item.title: Bitwarden CLI npm package compromised to steal developer credentials
item.description: The Bitwarden CLI was briefly compromised after attackers uploaded a malicious @bitwarden/cli package to npm containing a credential-stealing payload capable of spreading to other projects. [...]

item.title: Trigona ransomware attacks use custom exfiltration tool to steal data
item.description: Recently observed Trigona ransomware attacks are using a custom, command-line tool to steal data from compromised environments faster and more efficiently. [...]

item.title: @bitwarden/cli - GitGuardian Views on helloworm00
item.description: GitGuardian analysis of the @bitwarden/cli compromise: GitHub used as C2, new Cloudflare exfiltration domain found, linked to April 22 Checkmarx KICS compromise via Dependabot.

item.title: UNC6692 Impersonates IT Helpdesk via Microsoft Teams to Deploy SNOW Malware
item.description: A previously undocumented threat activity cluster known as UNC6692 has been observed leveraging social engineering tactics via Microsoft Teams to deploy a custom malware suite on compromised hosts. "As with many other...

item.title: It pays to be a forever student
item.description: In this newsletter, Joe discusses why understanding other disciplines can often flow back into the macro and micro of cybersecurity, especially in a world of AI.

item.title: Luxury cosmetics giant Rituals discloses data breach impacting member personal details
item.description: Rituals disclosed a breach where hackers accessed and downloaded some My Rituals members’ data, including names and addresses. Luxury cosmetics giant Rituals disclosed a data breach impacting My Rituals members after...

item.title: Google Favors General‑Purpose Gemini Models Over Cybersecurity‑Specific AI
item.description: Google Cloud’s COO advocated for combining general-purpose frontier large language models with task-specific AI agents

item.title: CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added one new vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2026-39987 Marimo Remote Code Execution Vulnerability This type of vulnerabilit...

item.title: New Checkmarx supply-chain breach affects KICS analysis tool
item.description: Hackers have compromised Docker images, VSCode and Open VSX extensions for the Checkmarx KICS analysis tool to harvest sensitive data from developer environments. [...]

item.title: What is Application Security?
item.description: Imagine you built a LEGO castle. It has walls, towers, gates, and hundreds of individual pieces working together. From the outside, it looks complete and secure. But if you look closer: Now imagine this castle is cons...

item.title: UAT-4356's Targeting of Cisco Firepower Devices
item.description: Cisco Talos is aware of UAT-4356's continued active targeting of Cisco Firepower devices’ Firepower eXtensible Operating System (FXOS). UAT-4356 exploited n-day vulnerabilities (CVE-2025-20333 and CVE-2025-20362) to g...

item.title: Cloudsmith Raises $72 Million in Series C Funding
item.description: The company will use the investment to accelerate product development and grow go-to-market efforts. The post Cloudsmith Raises $72 Million in Series C Funding appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Bad Memories Still Haunt AI Agents
item.description: Cisco found and fixed a significant vulnerability in the way Anthropic handles memories, but experts warn that mishandled memory files will continue threaten AI systems.

item.title: Cosmetics giant Rituals discloses data breach affecting customers
item.description: Dutch cosmetics giant Rituals disclosed a data breach after attackers stole the personal information of an undisclosed number of customers from its "My Rituals" membership database. [...]

item.title: Regular Password Resets Aren’t as Safe as You Think
item.description: Password resets are one of the easiest ways for attackers to bypass security controls. Specops Software shows how helpdesk social engineering turns a seemingly legitimate reset request into full account compromise. [...]

item.title: iOS Flaw Let Deleted Notifications Linger, Apple Issues Fix
item.description: Apple fixed an iOS flaw that kept deleted notifications on devices, allowing recovery of messages, including from apps like Signal. Apple released updates for iOS and iPadOS to address the vulnerability CVE-2026-28950...

item.title: Snow Flurries: How UNC6692 Employed Social Engineering to Deploy a Custom Malware Suite
item.description: Written by: JP Glab, Tufail Ahmed, Josh Kelley, Muhammad Umair Introduction Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) identified a multistage intrusion campaign by a newly tracked threat group, UNC6692, that leveraged p...

item.title: Bitwarden CLI Compromised in Ongoing Checkmarx Supply Chain Campaign
item.description: Bitwarden CLI has been compromised as part of the newly discovered and ongoing Checkmarx supply chain campaign, according to new findings from JFrog and Socket. "The affected package version appears to be @bitwarden/c...

item.title: AI is Changing Vulnerability Discovery and your Software Supply Chain Strategy has to Change with it
item.description: Wade Woolwine is Senior Director, Product Security at Rapid7. The headlines around Glasswing have focused on how quickly AI can surface vulnerabilities, which has naturally caught the attention of security leaders. In...

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: $290M DeFi Hack, macOS LotL Abuse, ProxySmart SIM Farms +25 New Stories
item.description: You scroll past one incident and see another that feels familiar, like it should have been fixed years ago, but it still works with small changes. Same bugs. Same mistakes. The supply chain is messy. Packages you did...

item.title: IP Fabric MCP server adds governance and control to enterprise AIOps workflows
item.description: IP Fabric has launched a new Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that removes key barriers to enterprise AIOps adoption, combining secure in-platform deployment with a built-in prompt library for network operations. W...

item.title: Apple Fixes iOS Notification Bug Exposing Deleted Messages
item.description: Apple patches iOS flaw that retained deleted notifications, exposing message data

item.title: Aqua Compass MCP server enables real-time investigation and containment of runtime threats
item.description: Aqua Security has announced Aqua Compass, a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that enables agentic investigation, containment and remediation of runtime incidents, and new runtime risk dashboards. These capabilities...

item.title: Chinese Cybersecurity Firm’s AI Hacking Claims Draw Comparisons to Claude Mythos
item.description: 360 Digital Security Group claims to have uncovered 1,000 vulnerabilities using AI, including at the Tianfu Cup hacking contest. The post Chinese Cybersecurity Firm’s AI Hacking Claims Draw Comparisons to Claude Mytho...

item.title: UK warns of Chinese hackers using proxy networks to evade detection
item.description: The United Kingdom's National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC-UK) and international partners warned that China-nexus hackers are increasingly using large-scale proxy networks of hijacked consumer devices to evade detectio...

item.title: New GopherWhisper APT group abuses Outlook, Slack, Discord for comms
item.description: A previously undocumented state-backed threat actor named GopherWhisper is using a Go-based custom toolkit and legitimate services like Microsoft 365 Outlook, Slack, and Discord in attacks against government entities....

item.title: [Webinar] Mythos Reality Check: Beating Automated Exploitation at AI Speed
item.description: Imagine a world where hackers don't sleep, don't take breaks, and find weak spots in your systems instantly. Well, that world is already here. Thanks to AI, attackers are now launching automated, large-scale exploits...

item.title: Google brings instant email verification to Android, no OTP needed
item.description: Google has introduced cryptographically verified email credentials for Android through the Credential Manager API. This API aligns with the W3C Digital Credential API standard. It provides a unified way for apps to re...

item.title: Google Introduces Unique AI Agent Identities in New Gemini Enterprise Platform
item.description: Google Cloud will attribute a unique cryptographic ID every AI agent that will be tied to “traceable and auditable” authorization policies

item.title: Rilian Raises $17.5 Million for AI-Native Security Orchestration
item.description: The company will hire new talent and expand operations across the US and other allied countries. The post Rilian Raises $17.5 Million for AI-Native Security Orchestration appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Project Glasswing Proved AI Can Find the Bugs. Who's Going to Fix Them?
item.description: Last week, Anthropic announced Project Glasswing, an AI model so effective at discovering software vulnerabilities that they took the extraordinary step of postponing its public release. Instead, the company has given...

item.title: If cyber espionage via HDMI worries you, NCSC built a device to stop it
item.description: A new cybersecurity device developed by the National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) should be a helpful solution for protecting governments and businesses from malicious activity carried through display connections. Cal...

item.title: Apple fixes iPhone bug that let FBI retrieve deleted Signal messages(CVE-2026-28950)
item.description: Apple has rolled out security updates for iPhones and iPads that fix CVE-2026-28950, a logging issue in Notification Services that made devices unexpectedly retain notifications marked for deletion. The vulnerability...

item.title: The Behavioral Shift: Why Trusted Relationships Are the Newest Attack Surface
item.description: New analysis from Abnormal AI reveals how attackers have abandoned technical exploits to weaponize routine workflows and internal trust. The post The Behavioral Shift: Why Trusted Relationships Are the Newest Attack S...

item.title: CISA orders feds to patch BlueHammer flaw exploited as zero-day
item.description: CISA has ordered U.S. federal agencies to patch a Microsoft Defender privilege escalation flaw (dubbed BlueHammer) that has been exploited in zero-day attacks. [...]

item.title: Luxury Cosmetics Giant Rituals Discloses Data Breach
item.description: The company is notifying My Rituals members that hackers downloaded part of their data, including names and addresses. The post Luxury Cosmetics Giant Rituals Discloses Data Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: RAMP Uncovered: Anatomy of Russia’s Ransomware Marketplace
item.description: Leaked data from RAMP reveals Russia’s ransomware ecosystem, analyzing 1,732 threads, 7,707 users, and 340,000 IP records from the forum. RAMP was not just another dark web forum. It was one of the clearest examples o...

item.title: Apple Patches Exploited Notification Flaw, (Thu, Apr 23rd)
item.description: Apple yesterday released iOS/iPadOS 26.4.2 and iOS/iPadOS 18.7.8. This update fixes a single Notification Services vulnerability, CVE-2026-28950:

item.title: AI Can Autonomously Hack Cloud Systems With Minimal Oversight: Researchers
item.description: Palo Alto Networks has developed Zealot, a multi-agent penetration testing PoC capable of reconnaissance, exploitation, and exfiltration. The post AI Can Autonomously Hack Cloud Systems With Minimal Oversight: Researc...

item.title: Can AI Attack the Cloud? Lessons From Building an Autonomous Cloud Offensive Multi-Agent System
item.description: Unit 42 reveals how multi-agent AI systems can autonomously attack cloud environments. Learn critical insights and vital lessons for proactive security. The post Can AI Attack the Cloud? Lessons From Building an Auton...

item.title: 'Zealot' Shows What AI's Capable of in Staged Cloud Attack
item.description: The proof of concept revealed AI-based attacks unfold too fast for human defenders to respond, and that AI evinced more autonomous behavior than expected.

item.title: No Off Season: Three Supply Chain Campaigns Hit npm, PyPI, and Docker Hub in 48 Hours
item.description: Three supply chain attacks hit npm, PyPI, and Docker Hub between April 21–23, 2026. All three targeted secrets: API keys, cloud credentials, SSH keys, and tokens from developer environments and CI/CD pipelines.

item.title: Researchers Uncover 10 In-the-Wild Prompt Injection Payloads Targeting AI Agents
item.description: Forcepoint has found 10 new indirect prompt injection attacks targeting AI agents

item.title: U.S. CISA adds a flaw in Microsoft Defender to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) adds a flaw in Microsoft Defender to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) added...

item.title: China-Linked GopherWhisper Infects 12 Mongolian Government Systems with Go Backdoors
item.description: Mongolian governmental institutions have emerged as the target of a previously undocumented China-aligned advanced persistent threat (APT) group tracked as GopherWhisper. "The group wields a wide array of tools mostly...

item.title: GopherWhisper APT group hides command and control traffic in Slack and Discord
item.description: Attackers continue to lean on everyday collaboration platforms to hide command and control traffic inside normal enterprise noise. A newly identified China-aligned APT group pushes that trend further, running its oper...

item.title: Apple Patches iOS Flaw Allowing Recovery of Deleted Chats
item.description: Apple rolled out the security patches for dozens of iPhone and iPad models and generations. The post Apple Patches iOS Flaw Allowing Recovery of Deleted Chats appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: OpenAI tackles a bad habit people have when interacting with AI
item.description: Since people tend to paste personal data into AI tools such as ChatGPT, OpenAI has released Privacy Filter, an open-weight model designed to detect and redact personally identifiable information (PII) in text. The mod...

item.title: Vercel Finds More Compromised Accounts in Context.ai-Linked Breach
item.description: Vercel on Wednesday revealed that it has identified an additional set of customer accounts that were compromised as part of a security incident that enabled unauthorized access to its internal systems. The company sai...

item.title: Apple Patches iOS Flaw That Stored Deleted Signal Notifications in FBI Forensic Case
item.description: Apple has rolled out a software fix for iOS and iPadOS to address a Notification Services flaw that stored notifications marked for deletion on the device. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-28950 (CVSS score: N/A...

item.title: Recent Microsoft Defender Vulnerability Exploited as Zero-Day
item.description: The flaw allows attackers to access the SAM database, extract NTLM hashes, and gain System privileges. The post Recent Microsoft Defender Vulnerability Exploited as Zero-Day appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Microsoft Graph API misused by new GoGra Linux malware for hidden communication
item.description: A new GoGra Linux malware uses Microsoft Graph API and an Outlook inbox to deliver payloads, making it stealthy and hard to detect. A new Linux version of the GoGra backdoor uses Microsoft’s Graph API and an Outlook i...

item.title: A year in, Zoom’s CISO reflects on balancing security and business
item.description: In this Help Net Security interview, Sandra McLeod, CISO at Zoom, reflects on her first year in the role. She talks about moving from reactive firefighting to business strategy, and what she heard from engineers, the...

item.title: Scenario: Open-source framework for automated AI app red-teaming
item.description: Enterprises running customer service bots, data analytics agents, and other AI-driven applications in production handle sensitive records and connect to core business systems every day. LangWatch has released Scenario...

item.title: GDPR works, but only where someone enforces it
item.description: A new measurement study of web tracking across ten countries offers a reality check for anyone working on privacy compliance. Researchers crawled the same set of globally popular websites from virtual machines located...

item.title: Ransomware, fraud, and lawsuits drive cyber insurance claims to new peaks
item.description: The 2026 InsurSec Report from At-Bay, covering more than 100,000 policy years of claims data, documents a 7% year-over-year rise in overall claim frequency and an all-time high average severity of $221,000. Ransomware...

item.title: ISC Stormcast For Thursday, April 23rd, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9904, (Thu, Apr 23rd)
Hardcoding Security into Every Commit: The Future of Snyk Secrets
item.description: Snyk Secrets bridges the gap between code and credentials with real-time, high-precision detection, ensuring your most sensitive data stays hidden while your developers stay fast.

item.title: JPMorgan Just Published a Cyber To-Do List and Snyk Covers 8 of the 10 Items. How do you stack up?
item.description: JPMorganChase published a 10-point cyber resilience checklist. See how Snyk covers 8 of the 10 actions and where it fits in your security stack.

item.title: Winter 2025 SOC 1 report is now available with 184 services in scope
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) is pleased to announce that the Winter 2025 System and Organization Controls (SOC) 1 report is now available. The report covers 184 services over the 12-month period from January 1, 2025 – De...

item.title: [Guest Diary] Beyond Cryptojacking: Telegram tdata as a Credential Harvesting Vector, Lessons from a Honeypot Incident, (Wed, Apr 22nd)
item.description: &#;x26;#;x5b;This is a Guest Diary by L. Carty, an ISC intern as part of the SANS.edu Bachelor&#;x26;#;39;s Degree in Applied Cybersecurity (BACS) program &#;x26;#;x5b;1].]

item.title: CrowdStrike Expands Real-Time Cloud Detection and Response to Google Cloud
CrowdStrike Falcon Cloud Security Delivered 264% ROI Through Unified Cloud Protection
CrowdStrike Expands Real-Time Cloud Detection and Response to Google Cloud
CrowdStrike Falcon Cloud Security Delivered 264% ROI Through Unified Cloud Protection
LABScon25 Replay | Are Your Chinese Cameras Spying For You Or On You?
item.description: Marc Rogers and Silas Cutler expose how cheap smart home devices conceal a shadow supply chain of shell companies, firmware flaws, and foreign data routing.

item.title: Apple fixes iOS bug that retained deleted notification data
item.description: Apple has released out-of-band security updates for iPhone and iPad devices to fix a Notification Services flaw that could allow notifications marked for deletion to remain stored on the device. [...]

item.title: 'The Gentlemen' Rapidly Rises to Ransomware Prominence
item.description: Not nearly as polite as the name suggests, the ransomware gang has impressed researchers with its speed in scaling up operations — and its sophistication.

item.title: New Mirai campaign exploits RCE flaw in EoL D-Link routers
item.description: A new Mirai-based malware campaign is actively exploiting CVE-2025-29635, a high-severity command-injection vulnerability affecting D-Link DIR-823X routers, to enlist devices into the botnet. [...]

item.title: Google’s Workspace Intelligence promises privacy while running on your data
item.description: Security and data governance are among the key considerations in Google’s latest AI update, which introduces Workspace Intelligence within Google Workspace. Google describes the feature as “a secure, dynamic system th...

item.title: CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added one new vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2026-33825 Microsoft Defender Insufficient Granularity of Access Control Vulner...

item.title: Kyber ransomware gang toys with post-quantum encryption on Windows
item.description: A new Kyber ransomware operation is targeting Windows systems and VMware ESXi endpoints in recent attacks, with one variant implementing Kyber1024 post-quantum encryption. [...]

item.title: DDoS wave continues as Mastodon hit after Bluesky incident
item.description: Mastodon suffered a major DDoS attack shortly after a similar incident hit Bluesky. The outage was significant but resolved within a few hours. Mastodon was hit by a major DDoS attack just days after a similar disrupt...

item.title: Malicious KICS Docker Images and VS Code Extensions Hit Checkmarx Supply Chain
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have warned of malicious images pushed to the official "checkmarx/kics" Docker Hub repository. In an alert published today, software supply chain security company Socket revealed that unknown...

item.title: Self-Propagating Supply Chain Worm Hijacks npm Packages to Steal Developer Tokens
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a fresh set of packages that have been compromised by bad actors to deliver a self-propagating worm that spreads through stolen developer npm tokens. The supply chain worm has be...

item.title: Mirai Botnet exploits CVE-2025-29635 to target legacy D-Link routers
item.description: Mirai botnet is targeting old D-Link routers using CVE-2025-29635, a command injection flaw exploitable via crafted POST requests after public PoC disclosure. A Mirai botnet is actively exploiting a command injection...

item.title: Cyberattack on French government agency triggers phishing alert
item.description: France Titres, a French government agency, has disclosed a data breach that may have exposed user data from its online portal. France Titres, also known as the Agence nationale des titres sécurisés (ANTS), operates un...

item.title: A technical walkthrough of multicloud full-stack security using AWS Security Hub Extended
item.description: Building on our recent announcement of AWS Security Hub Extended —our full-stack enterprise security offering — we want to show you how we’re simplifying security procurement and operations for your multicloud environ...

item.title: Harvester Deploys Linux GoGra Backdoor in South Asia Using Microsoft Graph API
item.description: The threat actor known as Harvester has been attributed to a new Linux version of its GoGra backdoor deployed as part of attacks likely targeting entities in South Asia. "The malware uses the legitimate Microsoft Grap...

item.title: NCSC Unveils SilentGlass, a Plug-In Device to Protect Monitors from Cyber-Attacks
item.description: The UK’s cybersecurity agency said the devices will be available for purchase by organizations around the world

item.title: DPRK Fake Job Scams Self-Propagate in 'Contagious Interview'
item.description: A compromised developer's repository serves as a worm-like infection vector to spread remote access Trojans (RATs) and other malware.

item.title: Claude Mythos finds 271 Firefox flaws, Mozilla believes it shifts security toward defenders
item.description: The Mozilla Foundation tested Claude Mythos, an Anthropic AI model that has stirred debate in the cybersecurity community. Before granting access to Mythos, Mozilla scanned Firefox using Opus 4.6, which led to fixes f...

item.title: After Bluesky, Mastodon Targeted in DDoS Attack
item.description: The DDoS attack caused a major outage, but Mastodon mitigated it within a few hours. The post After Bluesky, Mastodon Targeted in DDoS Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: UK Commits £90m for Cybersecurity and Pushes for ‘Resilience Pledge’
item.description: UK unveils £90m cybersecurity funding at CYBERUK to boost SME resilience, promote Cyber Essentials and a new Cyber Resilience Pledge, sparking industry debate

item.title: Inside Caller-as-a-Service Fraud: The Scam Economy Has a Hiring Process
item.description: Fraud operations now operate like call centers, complete with hiring, training, and performance tracking. Flare reveals how cybercriminals manage "Caller-as-a-Service" operations like a professional sales team. [...]

item.title: Microsoft out-of-band updates fixed critical ASP.NET Core privilege escalation flaw
item.description: Microsoft fixed critical ASP.NET Core vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-40372 (CVSS score of 9.1), that lets attackers escalate privileges. Microsoft released out-of-band updates to address a serious ASP.NET Core vul...

item.title: SnowFROC 2026: Secure Defaults, Real Trust, and a Better Layer on Top
item.description: This year's Devner OWASP event showed why modern AppSec depends on secure defaults, stronger provenance, and security controls that appear where developers make decisions.

item.title: Prove Identity Platform connects verification, authentication, and fraud prevention
item.description: Prove has launched the Prove Identity Platform, turning identity verification into an ongoing, real-time process for users, businesses, and AI agents. AI agents are already initiating real transactions on behalf of re...

item.title: Critical BRIDGE:BREAK flaws impact Lantronix and Silex Technology converters
item.description: 22 BRIDGE:BREAK flaws hit Lantronix and Silex Technology converters, exposing approximately 20,000 devices to hijacking and data tampering. Researchers at Forescout Research Vedere Labs found 22 BRIDGE:BREAK flaws in...

item.title: New Mirai variants target routers and DVRs in parallel campaigns
item.description: Hidden inside newly discovered botnet malware is an unusual message from its creator: “AI.NEEDS.TO.DIE”. Dubbed “tuxnokill” by researchers at Akamai, the malware is one of two fresh Mirai botnet variants documented th...

item.title: Acronis GenAI Protection gives MSPs control over AI usage and data risks
item.description: Acronis has launced Acronis GenAI Protection, a monitoring and security solution that enables managed service providers (MSPs) to control generative AI usage across client environments, preventing sensitive data expos...

item.title: Surge in Silent Subject Phishing Attacks Targets VIP Users
item.description: Null subject phishing campaigns bypass filters and target VIPs with QR code and RMM abuse

item.title: New npm supply-chain attack self-spreads to steal auth tokens
item.description: A new supply chain attack targeting the Node Package Manager (npm) ecosystem is stealing developer credentials and attempting to spread through packages published from compromised accounts. [...]

item.title: Most Serious Cyberattacks Against the UK Now From Russia, Iran and China, Cyber Chief Says
item.description: British businesses need to prepare themselves to defend against cyberattacks because the U.K. could be targeted “at scale,” if it became involved in an international conflict. The post Most Serious Cyberattacks Agains...

item.title: Elastic MCP Apps bring security and observability workflows into AI tools
item.description: Elastic has announced MCP Apps for Elastic, delivering agent-native UI experiences for security and observability workflows across third-party coding tools and chat clients. The new MCP Apps enable teams to investigat...

item.title: New Wiper Malware Targeted Venezuelan Energy Sector Prior to US Intervention
item.description: Dubbed Lotus Wiper, the malware targets recovery mechanisms, overwrites drives, and systematically deletes files. The post New Wiper Malware Targeted Venezuelan Energy Sector Prior to US Intervention appeared first on...

item.title: Wiz at Google Next: Machine-Speed Defense for Any Cloud, Any Platform, Any AI
item.description: Announcing new capabilities at Google Cloud Next that extend and deepen Wiz AI-APP coverage: From the first line of AI-generated code, across AI and agent studios, to the edge of the cloud

item.title: Mirai Botnet Targets Flaw in Discontinued D-Link Routers
item.description: The exploitation of the command injection vulnerability started one year after public disclosure and PoC exploit code publication. The post Mirai Botnet Targets Flaw in Discontinued D-Link Routers appeared first on Se...

item.title: Are SBOMs Failing? Supply Chain Attacks Rise as Security Teams Struggle With SBOM Data
item.description: Researcher says the missing piece is a governance-driven intelligence layer that turns SBOM and VEX data into explainable security decisions. The post Are SBOMs Failing? Supply Chain Attacks Rise as Security Teams Str...

item.title: Progress Software fixes sneaky WAF bypass vulnerability (CVE-2026-21876)
item.description: Progress Software has fixed a slew of high-severity vulnerabilities in MOVEit WAF and LoadMaster, including a flaw (CVE-2026-21876) that may allow attackers to bypass firewall detection. MOVEit WAF (web application fi...

item.title: Claude Mythos Finds 271 Firefox Vulnerabilities
item.description: All the flaws could have also been found by an elite human researcher, according to Mozilla. The post Claude Mythos Finds 271 Firefox Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Former Ransomware Negotiator Pleads Guilty to Working For BlackCat Cyber Gang
item.description: A former ransomware negotiator has pleaded guilty to abusing his position by working with noted cybercrime group BlackCat

item.title: Lotus Wiper Malware Targets Venezuelan Energy Systems in Destructive Attack
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a previously undocumented data wiper that has been used in attacks targeting Venezuela at the end of last year and the start of 2026. Dubbed Lotus Wiper, the novel file wiper...

item.title: North Korean Hackers Use AppleScript, ClickFix in Fresh macOS Attacks
item.description: The campaigns focus on financial organizations, including cryptocurrency, venture capital, and blockchain entities. The post North Korean Hackers Use AppleScript, ClickFix in Fresh macOS Attacks appeared first on Secu...

item.title: Toxic Combinations: When Cross-App Permissions Stack into Risk
item.description: On January 31, 2026, researchers disclosed that Moltbook, a social network built for AI agents, had left its database wide open, exposing 35,000 email addresses and 1.5 million agent API tokens across 770,000 active a...

item.title: Tencent’s QClaw AI agent app arrives on Windows and macOS
item.description: Tencent has opened an international beta of QClaw, an AI agent application aimed at consumers in Canada, Japan, Singapore, South Korea, and the United States. The first wave is capped at 20,000 users. Additional marke...

item.title: IR Trends Q1 2026: Phishing reemerges as top initial access vector, as attacks targeting public administration persist
item.description: Phishing reemerged as the most observed means of gaining initial access, accounting for over a third of the engagements where initial access could be determined. Phishing has not been the top vertical for initial acce...

item.title: When Wi-Fi Encryption Fails: Protecting Your Enterprise from AirSnitch Attacks
item.description: Unit 42 research reveals AirSnitch attacks bypass WPA2/3 Wi-Fi encryption and client isolation, exposing critical infrastructure vulnerabilities. The post When Wi-Fi Encryption Fails: Protecting Your Enterprise from A...

item.title: Phishing reclaims the top initial access spot, attackers experiment with AI tools
item.description: Phishing returned as the leading method attackers used to break into organizations in the first quarter of 2026, accounting for over a third of engagements where initial access could be determined, according to Cisco...

item.title: New GoGra malware for Linux uses Microsoft Graph API for comms
item.description: A Linux variant of the GoGra backdoor uses legitimate Microsoft infrastructure, relying on an Outlook inbox for stealthy payload delivery. [...]

item.title: Researchers Uncover ProxySmart Software Powering 90+ SIM Farms
item.description: Infrawatch says ProxySmart platform enables SIM farm activity at “industrial scale”

item.title: Google Antigravity in Crosshairs of Security Researchers, Cybercriminals
item.description: Researchers discovered a remote code execution vulnerability and cybercriminals are using its reputation to deliver malware. The post Google Antigravity in Crosshairs of Security Researchers, Cybercriminals appeared f...

item.title: OneDrive updates focus on AI, access control, and compliance
item.description: Microsoft OneDrive’s recent updates focus on improving intelligence, collaboration, and administrative control. “Last year, we made a promise: your files should work for you, not the other way around. That meant reima...

item.title: Microsoft Patches Critical ASP.NET Core CVE-2026-40372 Privilege Escalation Bug
item.description: Microsoft has released out-of-band updates to address a security vulnerability in ASP.NET Core that could allow an attacker to escalate privileges. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-40372, carries a CVSS score of...
